subject: UX & Research
title: The Apple Maps Fiasco
description: A 60-second lesson on The Apple Maps Fiasco.

hook.text: Apple replaced Google Maps with its own app in 2012. It directed drivers into rivers and across airport runways.

buildup.text: Apple Maps showed melted buildings, misplaced cities, and highways that didn't exist. Users were stranded.

discovery.text: Tim Cook publicly apologized and recommended users download Google Maps. Apple's CEO telling you to use a rival.

twist.text: Apple spent 8 years rebuilding Maps. Today it's competitive. But the launch reputation still haunts them.

climax.text: Shipping incomplete features to hit a deadline burns user trust. It's faster to delay than to recover reputation.

punchline.text: Don't ship broken core features. Trust is hard to rebuild.

quiz.question: What lesson did Apple Maps teach about product launches?
options: Shipping incomplete core features to hit a deadline destroys user trust
Map apps are inherently difficult to build
